edges.description: FLNB disruption of chondrocyte-driven endochondral ossification explains the severe disproportionate short-limb skeletal dysplasia.
Abnormal growth-plate chondrocyte function and endochondral bone growth produce the proximal limb shortening pattern.
FLNB-dependent joint morphogenesis and skeletal patterning defects plausibly lead to congenital large-joint dislocations.
Disrupted limb and joint morphogenesis produces congenital club-foot deformity through intermediate appendicular skeletal patterning defects.
Impaired chondrocyte endochondral ossification directly accounts for severe humeral underdevelopment.
FLNB involvement in vertebral development directly links the cytoskeletal signaling defect to coronal cleft vertebral abnormalities.
Vertebral segmentation and endochondral ossification abnormalities secondarily produce axial skeletal curvature.
FLNB-related skeletal morphogenesis disruption extends to craniofacial patterning, producing hypertelorism through developmental intermediates.
Craniofacial cartilage and bone patterning abnormalities downstream of FLNB dysfunction explain nasal bridge depression.
Disrupted craniofacial skeletal morphogenesis downstream of FLNB dysfunction accounts for mandibular hypoplasia.
Craniofacial developmental disruption downstream of FLNB dysfunction can perturb palatal morphogenesis.
Appendicular skeletal patterning defects downstream of FLNB dysfunction explain broad thumb morphology.
Abnormal cartilage development downstream of FLNB dysfunction can weaken tracheal cartilage support.
Airway malacia and severe skeletal dysplasia create respiratory compromise leading to apnea through intermediate respiratory mechanisms.
Severe skeletal dysplasia and respiratory compromise impair early motor development through multiple clinical intermediates.

nodes.description: Severe humeral underdevelopment is a striking radiographic feature.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination demonstrated striking hypoplasia of the humerus with proximal rounding and distal tapering giving a 'drumstick' appearance, 'S'-shape configuration of the cervical spine, scoliosis and coronal cleft in the thoracolumbar vertebral bodies.

nodes.description: Recurrent apnea has been documented in surviving infants with severe airway involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The infant experienced recurrent apnea and persistent severe tracheomalacia, which necessitated tracheostomy at 5 months of age.

nodes.description: Broad thumbs are part of the distal limb malformation pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The infant had short broad thumbs in the hands similar to those observed in the feet.

nodes.description: Cleft palate is part of the craniofacial spectrum in AOIII.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The patient had multiple craniofacial abnormalities at birth, including ocular hypertelorism, a flat nasal bridge, micrognathia and a cleft palate.

nodes.description: Coronal clefting of thoracolumbar vertebrae indicates severe vertebral dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination demonstrated striking hypoplasia of the humerus with proximal rounding and distal tapering giving a 'drumstick' appearance, 'S'-shape configuration of the cervical spine, scoliosis and coronal cleft in the thoracolumbar vertebral bodies.

nodes.description: Nasal bridge depression is part of AOIII facial dysmorphism.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The patient had multiple craniofacial abnormalities at birth, including ocular hypertelorism, a flat nasal bridge, micrognathia and a cleft palate.

nodes.description: AOIII is a severe short-limbed skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate limb shortening.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type III: a distinct skeletal dysplasia with features overlapping atelosteogenesis and oto-palato-digital syndrome type II.
evidence.snippet: We present 5 cases of a short-limb dwarfism syndrome whose manifestations overlap those of atelosteogenesis and oto-palato-digital syndrome Type II.

evidence.reference_title: FLNB-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet: FLNB-AO1 and FLNB-AO3 are characterized by severe short-limbed dwarfism; dislocated hips, knees, and elbows; and clubfeet.

nodes.description: Pathogenic FLNB variation disrupts filamin B cytoskeletal scaffolding functions, perturbing vertebral segmentation, joint morphogenesis, and chondrocyte-driven endochondral ossification.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding filamin B disrupt vertebral segmentation, joint formation and skeletogenesis.
evidence.snippet: We found that filamin B is expressed in human growth plate chondrocytes and in the developing vertebral bodies in the mouse.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding filamin B disrupt vertebral segmentation, joint formation and skeletogenesis.
evidence.snippet: These data indicate an unexpected role in vertebral segmentation, joint formation and endochondral ossification for this ubiquitously expressed cytoskeletal protein.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding filamin B disrupt vertebral segmentation, joint formation and skeletogenesis.
evidence.snippet: We identified mutations in the gene encoding filamin B in four human skeletal disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding filamin B disrupt vertebral segmentation, joint formation and skeletogenesis.
evidence.snippet: We found homozygosity or compound heterozygosity with respect to stop-codon mutations in autosomal recessive spondylocarpotarsal syndrome (SCT, OMIM 272460) and missense mutations in individuals with autosomal dominant Larsen syndrome (OMIM 150250) and the perinatal lethal atelosteogenesis I and III phenotypes (AOI, OMIM 108720; AOIII, OMIM 108721).

nodes.description: Ocular hypertelorism contributes to the characteristic craniofacial pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The patient had multiple craniofacial abnormalities at birth, including ocular hypertelorism, a flat nasal bridge, micrognathia and a cleft palate.

nodes.description: Congenital dislocations involving the hips, knees, and elbows are part of the core appendicular phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: AOI and AOIII are autosomal dominant lethal skeletal dysplasias characterized by overlapping clinical findings that include vertebral abnormalities, disharmonious skeletal maturation, hypoplastic long bones, and joint dislocations.

evidence.reference_title: FLNB-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet: FLNB-AO1 and FLNB-AO3 are characterized by severe short-limbed dwarfism; dislocated hips, knees, and elbows; and clubfeet.

nodes.description: Mandibular hypoplasia is a recurrent craniofacial abnormality.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: The patient had multiple craniofacial abnormalities at birth, including ocular hypertelorism, a flat nasal bridge, micrognathia and a cleft palate.

nodes.description: Motor developmental delay has been reported in a longer-term survivor, although published evidence does not establish whether it is primary or secondary to prolonged respiratory and orthopedic morbidity.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: Despite his multiple skeletal deformities and respiratory problems, this patient survived more than 1 year with motoneuronal developmental delay.

nodes.description: Rhizomelic shortening of the limbs is a key skeletal feature.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: There was rhizomelic shortness of the limbs and a club-foot.

nodes.description: Spinal curvature abnormality is part of the AOIII axial skeletal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Atelosteogenesis type 3: the first patient in Japan and a survivor for more than 1 year.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination demonstrated striking hypoplasia of the humerus with proximal rounding and distal tapering giving a 'drumstick' appearance, 'S'-shape configuration of the cervical spine, scoliosis and coronal cleft in the thoracolumbar vertebral bodies.

nodes.description: Club-foot deformity is present at birth.

evidence.snippet: There was rhizomelic shortness of the limbs and a club-foot.

nodes.description: Severe airway malacia contributes to recurrent respiratory compromise.
